# Updated files:

## components/Hero.tsx

- Changed the main content container to `flex flex-col` to strictly enforce vertical stacking of the Title and Description.

My mind is currently occupied with achieving a softer edge for the hero title background on mobile and tablet. Specifically, I'm examining `components/Hero.tsx` to integrate this fading blur into the current styles. The aim is to create a more integrated visual flow.


**Developing Background Masking**

I'm now thinking about how to apply a radial mask to the `h1` element. My initial concerns are that masking the `h1` directly might affect the sharpness of the text. So, I'm considering using a pseudo-element behind the `h1` to apply the mask and backdrop blur. I'm exploring the best approach to ensure a seamless fade-out effect. I'm now refining the masking approach by considering how to prevent the text from fading out with the background. My current strategy involves using a separate element, such as a pseudo-element or an absolute div, positioned behind the `h1` element. This involves negative margins on the background element, `relative z-10` on `h1`, and an absolute centered container behind the text, allowing `backdrop-blur` and the color `bg-black/10` to fade out, effectively getting a weaker blur on the edge of the text.
